title: Executive Order 10299—Designating an Additional Agency Pursuant to Section 103(a) of the Renegotiation Act of 1951

flags.justification: A narrow, specific designation bringing one agency under an existing statutory renegotiation framework.
This designates an agency as covered by an already-established statutory renegotiation framework rather than creating new standards.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing designation under an ongoing statutory program with its own defined scope.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
This designation is governed by the Renegotiation Act's own statutory review process.
A routine extension of an already-established profit-limitation and anti-profiteering framework to a specific additional agency.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Notable as an example of Congress institutionalizing anti-profiteering contract review as a standing statutory program (the Renegotiation Act) rather than relying on ad hoc executive-order provisions, echoing the same principle behind EO 9001's Title II profit caps during WWII.
